stories.headline: South Korea Bets $576 Billion on AI Chips, Tapping Samsung and SK Hynix to Cement Its HBM Lead
stories.key_takeaway: President Lee Jae Myung unveiled a $576 billion semiconductor and AI program anchored by 800 trillion won ($518B) in Samsung and SK Hynix fab investment, aiming to defend South Korea's dominance in HBM memory chips amid the global AI race.

pull_quote.text: We must secure the core elements of AI faster than any other country. Semiconductors, physical AI, and AI data centres are the triple axis for our great leap forward.

stories.article_excerpt: Standing alongside the chairmen of the world's two largest memory-chip makers, President Lee Jae Myung unveiled a $576 billion plan.

stories.headline: 'Tokenmaxxing' Is Over: Enterprises Pivot From Spend-at-All-Costs to Model-Routing Efficiency
stories.key_takeaway: Major enterprises like Uber, Meta and Amazon are abandoning unlimited 'tokenmaxxing' for cost discipline and model routing that can cut AI bills up to 90%, raising questions about the growth assumptions behind OpenAI and Anthropic's pre-IPO valuations.

pull_quote.text: Current growth rates for Anthropic and OpenAI are the fastest they will ever be, which is mostly a matter of basic math.

data_points.label: Uber monthly AI cap per employee
Time Uber burned its annual AI budget

stories.article_excerpt: Six months ago, the fastest way to look like an AI power user inside a big technology company was to burn tokens.

stories.headline: Lindy Moved 100% of Its Traffic Off Claude to DeepSeek — and Watched Its AI Bill Collapse
stories.key_takeaway: Lindy moved 100% of its production traffic from Anthropic's Claude to DeepSeek v4 to slash an inference bill that had grown larger than payroll, a cost-driven switch that signals the premium-model default is ending for the heaviest token consumers.

pull_quote.text: Pulled the trigger today and switched 100% of Lindy traffic to DeepSeek v4, churning from Anthropic models. Saves us millions of $ and we're actually seeing an increase in performance on many core use cases.

stories.article_excerpt: When Flo Crivello, the founder and CEO of AI agent startup Lindy, pulled the trigger last week, he did not move a slice.

stories.headline: IBM and Google Cloud Forge a Multi-Billion-Dollar Enterprise AI Alliance Built on Gemini
stories.key_takeaway: IBM and Google Cloud launched a multi-billion-dollar Google Cloud Practice staffing thousands of certified consultants to deploy Gemini-based AI agents across regulated industries, the latest move in a systems-integrator land grab to control enterprise AI deployment.

pull_quote.text: This partnership significantly expands the pool of expert Google Cloud consultants in the market to meet surging demand for AI.
pull_quote.role: President, Global Partner Ecosystem, Google Cloud

data_points.label: Stated opportunity in Google Cloud services
Banking, government, retail, telecom and more
Google Cloud partner agentic AI fund
NTT DATA Gemini experts and AI agents

stories.article_excerpt: Big Blue just placed a very large bet on a model it does not own. On June 4, IBM and Google Cloud announced a new practice.

stories.headline: Figure AI Enters 2026 as Humanoid Robotics' Best-Capitalized Bet — a $39B Valuation, Its Own AI Brain, and a Factory Race
stories.key_takeaway: The reported $48B Series D could not be verified; Figure AI's confirmed standing rests on a $1B-plus Series C at a $39B valuation that funds its Helix AI model and BotQ factory amid an intensifying, capital-heavy humanoid-robot race.

pull_quote.text: This milestone is critical to unlocking the next stage of growth for humanoid robots, scaling out our AI platform Helix and BotQ manufacturing.

stories.article_excerpt: When Figure AI told investors last autumn it had blown past $1 billion in committed capital at a $39 billion valuation.

stories.headline: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 as Three Tiers — Sol, Terra, and Luna — but You Cannot Use It Yet
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's record-setting GPT-5.6 ships as three durable tiers (Sol, Terra, Luna) but is locked to roughly 20 government-vetted partners under a new Trump cyber executive order, with broad availability only promised in the coming weeks.

pull_quote.text: At their request, we are starting with a limited preview for a small group of trusted partners whose participation has been shared with the government.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI on Friday unveiled its most capable model family to date — GPT-5.6, split into three permanent tiers.

stories.headline: Z.AI's GLM-5.2 Pushes Open-Weight Coding to the Frontier With a 1M-Token Context
stories.key_takeaway: Z.ai's MIT-licensed, 753B-parameter GLM-5.2 pairs a usable 1M-token context with near-frontier agentic-coding scores at a fraction of closed-model prices, making it the leading open-weight model and intensifying the Chinese open-weight surge.

pull_quote.text: It marks a substantial leap in long-horizon task capability over its predecessor GLM-5.1 and, for the first time, delivers that capability on a solid 1M-token context.

stories.article_excerpt: For two years the unspoken rule of the AI race was that the most capable coding models stayed locked behind a closed API.

stories.headline: Moonshot's Kimi K2.6 Bets on Open-Weight, Multimodal Agents for Coding and Visual Reasoning
stories.key_takeaway: Moonshot AI's open-weight Kimi K2.6 fuses native vision, trillion-parameter MoE scale, and 300-agent swarms into the most aggressive challenge yet to closed frontier models on long-horizon coding.

pull_quote.text: Vision is baked in architecturally, not bolted on.

stories.article_excerpt: When Moonshot AI open-sourced Kimi K2.6 on April 20, the Chinese lab did not just ship another large language model.

stories.headline: Alibaba's Compact Qwen3.5 Aims to Put Frontier-Class Reasoning on a Single GPU
stories.key_takeaway: Alibaba's open-weight Qwen3.5-9B delivers frontier-class reasoning and multimodal performance that beats models many times its size while running on a laptop, underscoring the industry's pivot from raw scale to efficiency.

pull_quote.text: How is this even possible?! Qwen has released 4 new models and the 9B is as good as GPT OSS 120b while being 13x smaller!
pull_quote.role: AI and tech educator, Blueshell AI

stories.article_excerpt: The smartest model you can run on a laptop this month was not built in California. It was built by Alibaba.

stories.headline: Anthropic's Economic Index Finds 35% of Workers Expect AI to Do Most of Their Job Within a Year
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's first survey linking Claude usage to worker beliefs found over 35% expect AI to do most of their job within a year, and the heaviest delegators are the most optimistic, not the most fearful.

pull_quote.text: Asked to forecast next year's capabilities, over 35% predicted that AI would be able to do most of their work.

data_points.label: Expect AI to do most work in 12 months
Chose a higher AI task-share for next year

stories.article_excerpt: Ask the people closest to the frontier of AI how much of their job a machine will be doing a year from now.

stories.headline: 'RoboAlign' Teaches Robot Policies to Reason at Test Time, Tightening Language-Action Alignment
stories.key_takeaway: RoboAlign makes vision-language-action models reason their way to an action and then grades that reasoning by whether the action succeeds, closing the language-action gap and beating supervised-fine-tuning baselines across simulated and real-robot benchmarks.

pull_quote.text: We attribute this discrepancy to the modality gap between language and low-level actions; optimizing embodied reasoning purely through language supervision does not guarantee improvements in actual action generation.
pull_quote.role: RoboAlign, KAIST / RLWRLD / Yonsei / UC Berkeley

data_points.label: Gain over SFT on a real robot

stories.article_excerpt: For the past two years, the dominant recipe for building robot brains has looked deceptively simple.

stories.headline: A New Benchmark Tests Whether AI Agents Can Reason in Real Time as the World Changes Around Them
stories.key_takeaway: A new open-source benchmark, the Real-Time Reasoning Gym, shows that even state-of-the-art LLM agents fail when an environment keeps evolving while they think, and that a parallel reaction-plus-planning architecture called AgileThinker beats single-mode agents under time pressure.

pull_quote.text: AgileThinker consistently outperforms agents engaging only one reasoning paradigm as the task difficulty and time pressure rise, effectively balancing reasoning depth and response latency.
pull_quote.role: Stanford, Tsinghua, Shanghai Jiao Tong, Georgia Tech

stories.article_excerpt: Remember your first highway drive: knuckles white on the wheel, eyes locked on the car ahead, when your exit suddenly nears.

stories.headline: US Partially Restores Anthropic's Claude Mythos 5 for Critical-Infrastructure Defenders — Fable 5 Stays Banned
stories.key_takeaway: A June 26 letter from Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick lets Anthropic redeploy its Mythos 5 cybersecurity model to roughly 100 vetted critical-infrastructure entities by waiving export-license rules, while Fable 5 and all non-approved users stay banned.

pull_quote.text: A license will no longer be required to export, reexport, or in-country transfer the Claude Mythos 5 Model to entities identified in Annex A to this letter and their foreign national employees, or to Anthropic's foreign national employees.

data_points.label: Orgs cleared for access via Annex A
Firms with Mythos access before the ban

stories.article_excerpt: Fifteen days after the federal government forced Anthropic to pull the plug on its two most powerful models worldwide.

stories.headline: The Government-Gated Era of Frontier AI Has Begun, and It Starts With GPT-5.6 and Mythos 5
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's voluntary GPT-5.6 preview and Anthropic's coerced Mythos 5 restoration both flow from Trump's June 2 executive order, establishing a new frontier-AI launch pattern of pre-release government review, vetted-partner access lists, and de facto federal delay authority.

pull_quote.text: The fact that the Department of the Treasury is the lead agency acting as a clearinghouse is deeply concerning. Neither AI nor cybersecurity are core competencies of the Treasury Department.
pull_quote.role: SVP for Policy, Institute for Security and Technology

data_points.label: Deadline to stand up the framework

stories.article_excerpt: For the past decade, the moment a frontier AI model shipped was the company's call alone.

stories.headline: As Its June 30 Effective Date Arrives, Colorado's Landmark AI Act Is Already Hobbled by a Hold on Enforcement
stories.key_takeaway: Colorado's first-in-the-nation high-risk AI law takes effect June 30, 2026 but a magistrate's order bars enforcement until rulemaking is finished, while an xAI lawsuit, a Trump DOJ preemption push and a state-passed rewrite have already gutted it.

pull_quote.text: The court order buys time on enforcement, not on obligations. The June 30 deadline is still on the books.

data_points.label: Trump EO targeting state AI laws

stories.article_excerpt: On Tuesday, the first comprehensive 'high-risk' artificial intelligence statute in the United States is supposed to take effect.
